title: picea asperata var. retroflexa
intro: Picea asperata var. retroflexa, also known as the Tibetan spruce, is a variant of the Picea asperata tree species, native to the mountainous regions of China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the essential oils and compounds present in its needles. The health benefits of Picea asperata var. retroflexa include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Picea asperata var. retroflexa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and immune-related conditions.
